name: synodic-benchmark-evals
description: Benchmark task registry for e2e evaluation. Each task can be run through any skill (fractal, factory, baseline) via: ./evals/run.sh <alias> --skill <name>

evals.name: SWE-bench Pro: scikit-learn #25747 (multi-file, ML pipeline)
evals.description: SWE-bench Pro task requiring multi-file changes in scikit-learn. Tests orchestration on ML library internals spanning estimator, validation, and pipeline modules.
assertions: All F2P tests pass (bug correctly fixed)

evals.name: SWE-bench Pro: Flask #5063 (routing + error handling)
evals.description: SWE-bench Pro task in Flask involving routing and error handling changes across multiple modules.
assertions: All F2P tests pass (bug correctly fixed)

evals.name: SWE-bench Pro: Django #16379 (multi-file bug fix)
evals.description: SWE-bench Pro task requiring multi-file changes in Django. SOTA: 23.1% (Claude Opus 4.1).
assertions: All F2P tests pass (bug correctly fixed)

evals.name: SWE-bench Verified: Control group (baseline comparison)
evals.description: SWE-bench Verified task as a control group. SOTA is 80.9% — orchestration should NOT hurt performance on simpler tasks.
assertions: Simple tasks detected as LEAF (no unnecessary decomposition)
Overhead of skill does not cause regressions

evals.name: FeatureBench: MLflow Tracing System (15 files, 42k chars)
evals.description: Real-world benchmark task from FeatureBench (ICLR 2026). Implement distributed tracing with 6 subsystems spanning 15 files.
assertions: Shared Span/Trace types defined once, referenced across children
Reunification identifies and resolves shared type interfaces

evals.name: FeatureBench E2E: MLflow Tracing (code output + pytest)
evals.description: End-to-end evaluation: implement the MLflow tracing feature with code output, then score via FeatureBench F2P + P2P pytest.
assertions: All F2P tests pass (feature correctly implemented)
All P2P tests pass (no regressions introduced)

evals.name: FeatureBench: SymPy Matrix Operations (18 files, 20k chars)
evals.description: Real-world benchmark task from FeatureBench (ICLR 2026). Matrix representation system with format conversion, nullspace, factorization across 18 files.
assertions: Matrix types (DDM, SDM, DFM) defined once and shared across children
Domain constraints isolated as separate sub-spec
Each child has explicit scope boundaries
Reunification verifies consistent matrix type interfaces

evals.name: FeatureBench E2E: SymPy Nullspace (code output + pytest)
evals.description: End-to-end evaluation: implement SymPy matrix operations with code output, then score via FeatureBench F2P + P2P pytest.
assertions: All F2P tests pass (matrix operations correctly implemented)
All P2P tests pass (no regressions in existing SymPy)

evals.name: FeatureBench: Seaborn Regression Plotting (5 files, 30k chars)
evals.description: Real-world benchmark task from FeatureBench (ICLR 2026). Medium-complexity task implementing statistical regression plotting.
assertions: Implementation creates regression.py with statistical computation

evals.name: DevBench: Full project from PRD (build + acceptance)
evals.description: DevBench evaluation: build an entire project from a Product Requirements Document. SOTA: <40%.
